title: <div>mom emptying snack cabinets in my backpack — parents don’t realize that the
content:
mom emptying snack cabinets in my backpack — parents don’t realize that they are actually abusing their children (allowing a child to become obese, or being in a toxic relationship causes great trauma) — eggshell walk-ing

——————————————————————
A: when are you taking that drug test?

M: the answer hasn’t changed since the 98th time you asked me yesterday: I don’t know.

A: well it is time to take some initiative, it’s been 4 weeks since your interviewed and thus have been sober. You’re letting me down.

M: I’m letting you down? I’m letting me down, goddammit. Can you get me those other things from your sister?

A: Yeah sure, that’s fine…though, I’d be remiss, even though who am I to talk, but nevertheless I’d be remiss if I didn’t tell you to be careful. I watched two of my closest friends back home fall down the drain of addiction. I mean, one friend started to wear a bandana.

M: I know, I know. Its just, I hate being sober.

A: Yeah, same. Its crazy how it is acceptable to take those drugs but frowned upon to smoke.
context: From document: Web Sitcom Ideas — chunk 3 (chunk 3/798)
